creator: bugzilla.freebsd@omnilan.de
text:
Sorry for kind of hijacking the original problem report.
To my understanding this still is an issue.

For unexpected debugging purposes, I had to replicate a quiet complicated setup.
It's is a productive FreeBSD stable/13 setup, which doesn't contain the compiler nor toolchain and includes.

Now I have a identical setup in my lab, and I expected adding src and obj datasets and llvm12 from ports would allow me to build parts of the stable/13 system (kernel).

But this fails exaclty for the reasons already reported here (build(7) depends on base includes, wich are intentionally not part of the base system and should be searched from src instead of base).
How can I compile FreeBSD on FreeBSD without base toolchain?

Thanks,
-harry
